core_mechanism: lifting a nonlinear invertible transformation to the linear composition (Koopman) operator on a space of observables, so that equivalence (conjugacy) of two transformations is detected via a shared eigenfunction of their associated linear operators, with the equivalence map recovered as the limit of an iterated fixed-point sequence
brief_reason: The abstract relates the existence of a conjugacy between two homeomorphisms to a common generalized eigenfunction of the associated Koopman operators and constructs the conjugacy as the limit of a sequence generated by a conjugacy operator, which is exactly the Koopman linearization-plus-shared-eigenfunction machinery.
